description: Known biases present in the dataset that may affect fairness, representativeness, or model performance. Uses BiasTypeEnum for standardized bias categorization mapped to the AI Ontology (AIO).
Known limitations of the dataset that may affect its use or interpretation. Distinct from biases (systematic errors) and anomalies (data quality issues).
Compensation or incentives provided to human research participants.
Information about protections for vulnerable populations (e.g., minors, pregnant women, prisoners) including special safeguards and assent procedures.
Data imputation methodology and techniques.
Analysis of annotation quality and inter-annotator agreement.
Explicit intended and recommended uses for this dataset. Complements future_use_impacts by focusing on positive applications.
Explicitly prohibited or forbidden uses for this dataset. Stronger than discouraged_uses - these are not permitted.
Steps taken or recommended to mitigate this bias.
Indicates whether any confidential data elements are present.
Indicates whether any content warnings are needed.
Indicates whether sensitive data elements are present.
Strategy used to handle missing data (e.g., deletion, imputation, flagging, multiple imputation).
Annotation methodology, tasks, and protocols followed during labeling. Includes annotation guidelines, quality control procedures, and task definitions.
Specific imputation technique used (mean, median, mode, forward fill, backward fill, interpolation, model-based imputation, etc.).
Fields or columns where imputation was applied.
Justification for the imputation approach chosen, including assumptions made about missing data mechanisms.
Methods used to validate imputation quality (if any).
Methodology used to assess annotation quality and resolve disagreements.
Reason why this use is prohibited (e.g., license restriction, ethical concern, privacy risk, legal constraint).
How often updates are planned (e.g., quarterly, annually).
Time period for data retention.
What specific uses did participants consent to? Are there limitations on data use based on consent?
Were participants compensated for their participation?
What type of compensation was provided (e.g., monetary payment, gift cards, course credit, other incentives)?
What was the amount or value of compensation provided? Include currency or equivalent value.
What was the rationale for the compensation structure? How was the amount determined to be appropriate?
Are any vulnerable populations included (e.g., children, pregnant women, prisoners, cognitively impaired individuals)?
What additional protections were implemented for vulnerable populations? Include safeguards, modified procedures, or additional oversight.
Confidentiality classification of the dataset indicating level of access restrictions and sensitivity.
Indicates whether this variable contains sensitive information (e.g., personal data, protected health information).
